programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Vanderbilt battles Tennessee for state bragging rights in an SEC Tournament quarterfinal game. These teams split a pair of regular season matches, with each scoring a four-point win on the other team's floor.

programme.desc: From Charles Schwab Field Omaha in Omaha, Neb.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: John Calipari begins his second season with the No. 14 Razorbacks as they host the Jaguars. Calipari looks to build off Arkansas' Sweet Sixteen run last season, and added two top NBA Draft prospects in freshman guards Darius Acuff and Meleek Thomas.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 6 Cardinals play the No. 25 Razorbacks in the SEC/ACC Challenge. Louisville is 7-0 for the first time since 2021 after beating NJIT by 57. Arkansas is 2-0 all-time at the Challenge. Hogs freshman Meleek Thomas averages 17.6 points per game.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 15 Arkansas (11-3) travels to Neville Arena for an SEC clash with Auburn. Freshman Darius Acuff Jr. (19.5 PPG) leads the Hogs, while Keyshawn Hall (20.1 PPG) counters for the Tigers. Auburn aims to recover from an OT loss to Georgia.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 17 Razorbacks travel to Athens to take on the No. 21 Bulldogs. Georgia's Somtochukwu Cyril has been a revelation, averaging 2.9 blocks and 6.1 rebounds per game. Arkansas' Darius Acuff Jr. is one of the nation's premier scorers with 19.9 PPG.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Tyler Tanner leads No. 10 Vanderbilt against No. 17 Arkansas at Bud Walton Arena. The Razorbacks look to utilize Trevon Brazile's interior defense against Devin McGlockton and a surging Commodores squad in a battle for SEC supremacy.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: From Bud Walton Arena in Fayetteville, Ark.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: A battle of elite guards highlights the ice in Starkville as the No.21 Arkansas Razorbacks faces the Mississippi State Bulldogs. The Razorbacks look to maintain their top-four SEC standing, while the Bulldogs aim for a signature home win.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 21 Arkansas visits LSU at the Pete Maravich Assembly Center. The Razorbacks won the Jan. 25 meeting 76-73 behind 31 points from freshman Darius Acuff Jr. The Tigers now rely on senior Max Mackinnon to defend home court in this SEC showdown.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 21 Arkansas hosts Auburn at Bud Walton Arena. Razorback Darius Acuff Jr. headlines against Keyshawn Hall. D. Acuff Jr. shoots 49% from the field, while Hall leads the Tigers. Both teams battle for a top spot in the competitive SEC standings.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 21 Arkansas Razorbacks travel to Tuscaloosa to face the Alabama Crimson Tide in a marquee SEC clash. Alabama's sophomore sensation Labaron Philon Jr. and Arkansas's Darius Acuff Jr. are both among the league's top scorers.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Missouri travels to Fayetteville to take on No. 20 Arkansas. Arkansas sits near the top of the SEC standings, while Missouri competes for positioning in the middle of the rankings. Darius Acuff Jr. leads Arkansas in scoring and assists.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 20 Arkansas (20-7) hosts Texas A&M (19-8) at Bud Walton Arena. The Aggies seek a third straight win after beating Oklahoma 75-71. John Calipari and the Razorbacks look to high scorer Darius Acuff Jr. to lead the home offense in the SEC matchup.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.desc: Jon tackles spring's toughest challenge: catching walleyes under bright skies and high pressure. He details how small tweaks in cadence, line, and lure color can turn the tide.
